description: "Fleet GitOps YAML linter, language server, and migration tool for validating and upgrading Fleet configurations."

# flint — Fleet GitOps YAML Linter & Language Server

flint is installed on this system. Use it for linting, migrating, and
editing Fleet GitOps YAML configurations.

## When to use flint

Trigger this skill when the user mentions:
- Fleet GitOps YAML validation, linting, or fixing
- Migrating a Fleet repo to a new version (teams->fleets, queries->reports)
- Setting up an editor (VS Code, Neovim, Zed, Sublime, JetBrains)
- Deprecated Fleet keys or directory structure
- Fleet policies, reports, labels, controls, software, agent_options
- .fleetlint.toml configuration

## How to use

1. Run `flint help-ai` for the command index and SOP routing
2. Match user intent to the right SOP:
   - Lint/validate YAML -> `flint help-ai --sop lint`
   - Migrate to new Fleet version -> `flint help-ai --sop migrate`
   - Set up editor -> `flint help-ai --sop lsp`
3. Run `flint help-ai --command <name>` for specific command flags
4. Use `--format json` for machine-readable output

## Key commands

```
flint check <path>                            # lint YAML files
flint check <path> --fix                      # auto-fix safe issues
flint check <path> --format json              # structured output
flint migrate <path> --target-version 4.85.0  # migration report (JSON)
flint init                                    # create .fleetlint.toml
flint list-rules --format json                # all available rules
flint help-ai --sop migrate                   # migration SOP
flint help-json                               # full CLI schema as JSON
```

## Important patterns

- Commands use SPACES: `flint help-ai` (NOT `flint help.ai`)
- Dots are ONLY for `--command` lookups: `flint help-ai --command check`
- `--format json` on check and list-rules for structured output
- `--fix` auto-applies safe fixes, `--unsafe-fixes` for risky ones
- `flint migrate` outputs JSON report -- does NOT apply changes
- The LSP (`flint lsp`) powers all editor extensions
- `.fleetlint.toml` configures rules, thresholds, and Fleet connection

## What flint validates (always available, no network needed)

- Required keys per file type (default.yml, fleet YAML, unassigned)
- Deprecated keys with version-gated severity (warning -> error -> removed)
- osquery platform compatibility (100+ tables across darwin, windows, linux)
- SQL syntax validation for queries
- Structural validation (unknown keys, typo detection with suggestions)
- Label consistency (targeting, membership, referenced = defined)
- Date formats, hash formats, file extensions
- Secret hygiene (no hardcoded tokens in webhook URLs)
